verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1/p24d1) is a member of the p24 family of type-I transmembrane cargo receptors. It localizes to COPI and COPII vesicles, the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC), and the Golgi stack, functioning in cargo selection and vesicular transport within the early secretory pathway. Its steady-state distribution is in endomembrane compartments (ER, ERGIC, cis-Golgi), not the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Checking all contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death ecto-pool for this protein; no tissue-restricted outer-leaflet display; not a lysosomal TM protein subject to exocytosis; no documented PM minority pool or trafficking cycling to the plasma membrane surface; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the outer leaflet. No surface-directed clinical therapeutic program is known. Its TM domain is embedded in the endomembrane system, not the plasma membrane outer leaflet.
